description: Strong cloth prepared or stretched as a surface for painting is 'phanu'. It may be loose, rolled, or held on a frame. 'liru' (fabric) is the broader material; a source record carries the fibre, weave, coating, and dimensions when those details matter.
articulatory_notes: Begin 'pha' by narrowing the lips without sealing them, so unvoiced breath opens into stressed central 'a'. For 'nu', rest the tongue tip behind the upper teeth and carry the voiced dental nasal into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The opening breath spreads into broad 'a' before final 'nu' gathers the voice behind a dental contact. The widening and gathering suit cloth drawn taut across a frame.
usage_notes: Canvas may be counted as a prepared or framed surface, while canvas as material needs no count. A work made on it keeps the name of its making rather than acquiring another lexical sense of 'phanu'.

examples.translation: I decorate the canvas with a brush.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Canvas carries drawn line and colour while its weave remains part of the visible surface. Ornament gains a material grain.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a prepared surface whose texture enters the appearance of a painted work
semantic_domains.creation: cloth arranged to receive pigment during painting
semantic_domains.physical: strong fabric used loose, rolled, or stretched on a frame
